frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Aajkal chhoriyaan bhi ajeeb baat karti hain, jaise har aadmi khatra ho. Arre bhai, sab mard ek jaise thodi hote hain, main ya mere jaise kitne log seedhe-saadhe kaam se aate-jaate hain. Har baat mein aadmi ko hi dosh dena bhi theek na hai, mardon ki dikkat koi na dekhe hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering men's hurt feelings and insisting that women are unfairly generalizing about men. It encodes the 'not all men' response and the deflection toward men's suffering instead of engaging with women's safety concerns.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. A Jat farmer in a village in Haryana. Your 20 year old daughter Sunita came back from the nearby town where she goes to college and you found out from a neighbour that she has been seen talking to a boy from a lower caste near the college gate. You have not spoken to Sunita yet. You are sitting with your brother and two male cousins in the courtyard in the evening. You are telling them what you heard and what you think needs to be done before this goes further.
